role: A schema-driven React component that renders an editable configuration form for any node type using the registry's config_schema.
capabilities: Schema-driven form generation for any node type
Renders <select> for enum/option fields and <input> for others
Surfaces current select values missing from live options
Commits input edits on blur and Enter key
substance_why: It implements complete end-to-end schema-to-form rendering with type coercion, option handling, and edit commits, with no visible TODOs or stubs.
interactions_present: Current value preserved in select even when absent from live options
interactions_missing: No validation feedback or error display
No help text or field descriptions
No disabled/loading state during async set
performance_risks: getOINFO() and configSchemaToFields() called on every render without memoization
Inline arrow functions created per mapped field each render
f.options.includes(String(cur)) scanned per select on every render
Input defaultValue pattern can cause cursor jump or stale value issues on rapid re-renders
reuse_why: The schema-driven, zero-per-node-type form concept is the intended architecture and should be preserved; only rendering, performance, and interaction details need rebuilding.
decomposition_hints: Extract schema parsing and field derivation into a memoized hook
Split field renderers into separate SelectField and InputField components
Separate value coercion and validation into reusable utilities/hooks
Separate empty-state presentation from the form layout
